silent_assumption_identified: The user is implicitly framing the PRL as 'a methodologically clever extension of swift.jl in R-space' — most naturally the 4N Yakubovsky leap or realistic-NN n+d above-breakup. This frames swift.jl as the headline. The contrarian read is that R-space Faddeev with realistic NN above 3N breakup is a PRC-tier engineering claim (Deltuva-Fonseca already own the realistic-NN above-4N-breakup story in P-space), and a true PRL must instead use swift.jl as ONE LEG of a physics punchline whose broad-significance comes from somewhere else (a chiral-3NF constraint, a halo / Borromean dissociation observable, a cross-line bridge to Line A or Line F, or a quantum-information / wave-function-sensitivity claim that is not visible in phase shifts). The frame to break: 'swift.jl is the protagonist of the abstract.' Often it should be the witness.

candidates.one_line: Use swift.jl as ground truth for an AV18 vs AV18pq wave-function-sensitive observable at A=3 that phase shifts cannot distinguish, settling a 23-year-old NN off-shell ambiguity.
candidates.silent_assumption_violated: Assumes the PRL must be a method paper; this is a physics-constraint paper where swift.jl is the witness, not the headline.
candidates.prl_handle: I identify a low-energy 3N observable (n-d analyzing-power tensor combination or sub-threshold A_y at specific kinematics) that responds to NN short-range off-shell behavior despite identical on-shell phase shifts, providing the first model-independent experimental discriminator between phase-equivalent NN interactions and resolving the AV18 / AV18pq degeneracy posed by Wiringa, Arriaga, Pandharipande in 2003.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Today: compute n+d elastic A_y(θ) at E_n = 3 MeV with both AV18 and a manually-cooked AV18-like potential whose short-range wave function has been deformed (e.g. multiply by f(r) = 1 + α·exp(-r²/b²) with α tuned to preserve δ_l to 4 digits) using swift.jl in the existing MT pipeline scaled up; check whether the 3N observable differs at the 5% level. One day of swift.jl runs, no new code.
candidates.cost_estimate: month 1: AV18 vs phase-equivalent-deformation A=3 observable scan (swift.jl, existing infrastructure); month 2-4: extend to realistic AV18pq family (Wiringa 2003 explicitly gave the deformations), and to T_20, polarization-transfer observables; month 5-7: chiral-EFT N3LO/N4LO families (LENPIC, Idaho) as a second test of universality of the discriminator; month 8-10: send to Bochum (Witala) / Pisa (Kievsky-Viviani) for independent confirmation, identify the experimental kinematic sweet spot at TUNL or RCNP; month 11-12: write.

info_gain.if_succeeds: First model-independent discriminator between phase-equivalent NN potentials. Punchline matters to the entire nuclear-structure community (off-shell ambiguity affects every ab initio calculation), and the experimental community gets a concrete kinematic target. PRL clears the broad-significance bar because it answers a famous open question, not because it benchmarks a code.
info_gain.if_fails: If no observable separates them at A=3, that itself is a PRC-tier statement of robustness of universal short-range physics. Fallback paper: 'A=3 robustness of phase-equivalent NN ambiguity, with implications for what A>3 observables to chase next' — directly seeds a Line E follow-up emulator paper to map the off-shell sensitivity systematically.

candidates.risk: Witala / Glöckle / Kievsky may have buried partial answers in 2000s-era n+d analyzing-power papers (Ay puzzle literature). Need a literature-search to confirm this is genuinely open. Risk that the discriminator is too small to measure — but the negative result is still PRC-worthy. Low scoop risk because no one is currently framing this as the Wiringa-2003 follow-up.
candidates.orthogonality_to_tried: Not T1 (P-space, structural universality, not phase-equivalent ambiguity). Not T2 (bound state only, this is scattering). Not T3 (T3 is realistic NN demo with MT; this requires REAL AV18 above-breakup, different physics question). Not T4-T8 (entirely different research line). Not T11 (4N Yakubovsky leap is avoided).
candidates.orthogonality_to_already_read: Goes beyond Wiringa 2003 by identifying the discriminating observable, not just demonstrating non-uniqueness. Goes beyond Lazauskas-Carbonell 2011 by asking a physics question with the code, not benchmarking the code. Distinct from Zhang-Furnstahl emulator agenda. Not in Kato CSGF territory.
candidates.dependencies: swift.jl realistic-NN n+d above-breakup pipeline (T3 needs to be upgraded from MT to AV18 anyway). AV18pq Fortran kernel (already available via Wiringa group). Cross-check via Kievsky-Viviani PHH code (collaborator request).
candidates.competitor_scoop_window: Bochum group (Witala-Skibinski-Topolnicki) has the closest infrastructure to ask this question but they have not framed it as the Wiringa 2003 follow-up in any 2024-2026 paper Jin has read. Estimated head-start if they pivot now: 0-3 months. Pisa (Kievsky-Viviani-Marcucci) has PHH machinery but is focused on chiral 3NF mapping, not phase-equivalent off-shell. Deltuva not in this space.

candidates.one_line: PRL claim: the same NEB / Line A flux that escapes into the breakup channel is exactly accounted for by the swift.jl Faddeev 3-body wave function in a Borromean projectile reaction, unifying IAV inclusive breakup with ab initio 4-body CDCC.
candidates.silent_assumption_violated: Assumes swift.jl and Line A (IAV / CDCC) live in different worlds. Diagonal move: make the PRL a bridge paper between Jin's two largest research lines, demonstrating that what IAV calls 'NEB cross section' is identical to what configuration-space Faddeev calls 'breakup flux' for a specific Borromean test case — and that the IAV approximation error is computable.
candidates.prl_handle: I demonstrate that the inclusive non-elastic breakup cross section, defined operationally in the IAV / Ichimura-Austern-Vincent framework for 20 years, equals the exact Faddeev 3-body breakup flux for a Borromean projectile up to a quantifiable approximation error of less than 10%, thereby placing the entire NuPECC 4b-IAV / 4-body-CDCC program on a microscopic foundation.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Today: pick the simplest tractable Borromean case (⁶He + target at sub-Coulomb energy, or even ²H+α as a non-Borromean control), compute the breakup cross section both via SMOOTHIE (Lei + Liu Hao daily-driver IAV code) and via swift.jl at the bound-state-flux-into-breakup level, and check whether the orders-of-magnitude match. One day of running both codes on existing infrastructure.
candidates.cost_estimate: month 1-2: tractable benchmark (²H+α or ⁶Li-as-d+α breakup at sub-Coulomb, where swift.jl can solve it exactly and SMOOTHIE has 14-paper track record); month 3-6: Borromean test (⁶He + target) — needs ⁴-body-CDCC formulation of NEB; month 7-10: identify the kinematic window where the IAV approximation breaks; month 11-12: write.

info_gain.if_succeeds: Lei's two largest research lines fuse into a single PRL with concrete prediction power. Punchline matters to the entire breakup / RIB experimental community (FRIB, RIBLL, GANIL, RIKEN), because it gives them a calibrated approximation error on the universally-used IAV formula. Single-author potential since Lei owns both code lines.
info_gain.if_fails: Even a partial result — 'the IAV approximation has X% error in this regime, computed via Faddeev' — is a high-impact PRC and the natural Line A method paper Lei was already planning. Builds the bridge Liu Hao's thesis needs.

candidates.risk: Full Borromean 4-body CDCC is the NuPECC 2024 priority but its infrastructure cost is high — Lei has SMOOTHIE for 3-body CDCC and swift.jl for 3-body Faddeev, but a true 4-body benchmark requires either Yakubovsky or 4-body CDCC. The medium-risk fallback is the ⁶Li-as-d+α 3-body benchmark which is fully in-budget but less dramatic. Moro / Rodriguez-Gallardo (Sevilla) could scoop the 4-body CDCC half independently.
candidates.orthogonality_to_tried: Not T4 (T4 is the IAV-DWBA line; this is the IAV-vs-Faddeev disambiguation, never published). Not T7-T10 (different research line). Bridges T2 / T3 (swift.jl) with T4 (IAV) which has never been done.
candidates.orthogonality_to_already_read: Lazauskas-Carbonell 2013 review names 4-body CDCC for Borromean as the open NuPECC item but does not bridge to IAV. The IAV community (Moro-Lei trilogy) has never microscopically validated the IAV approximation against full Faddeev. This is a genuine cross-community paper.
candidates.dependencies: Liu Hao (SMOOTHIE), Moro (Sevilla, IAV formalism authority), swift.jl realistic-NN 3-body Faddeev with charged channels (extensions of current MT pipeline).
candidates.competitor_scoop_window: Moro (Sevilla) himself could scoop the 4-body CDCC half, but he doesn't run a Faddeev code, so the bridge requires Lei. Rodriguez-Gallardo (Sevilla) has 4-body CDCC infrastructure but no IAV. The cross-pollination is uniquely Lei's. 6-9 month safety window.

candidates.one_line: Use swift.jl + Line E emulator to deliver the first Bayesian posterior on a chiral-3NF low-energy constant from a 3N scattering observable, beating Bochum / Pisa to the full-3N-scattering-with-Bayesian-UQ milestone.
candidates.silent_assumption_violated: Assumes the PRL handle is the Faddeev solve itself. The handle is the BAYESIAN POSTERIOR — Lei already owns the bound-state-basis emulator (Line E, PRC 113, 024614 + 044610), and the punchline matters because the chiral-EFT community has been demanding scattering-observable Bayesian UQ since König 2020 (bound state only) and Zhang-Furnstahl 2022 (1-parameter only).
candidates.prl_handle: I report the first ab initio Bayesian posterior on the chiral-EFT cD low-energy constant from n-d analyzing-power data above 3N breakup threshold, using a reduced-basis emulator over the chiral-3NF parameter space that solves the configuration-space Faddeev equations to 0.5% accuracy in seconds per posterior sample.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Today: run the existing SLAM.jl + DBMM emulator (PRC 113, 044610 infrastructure) on a 2-parameter chiral-3NF mockup (cD, cE varied over their König 2020 prior range) at the swift.jl n+d level with MT potential as a smoke test, verify the emulator-vs-truth agreement is sub-percent on a single observable. One day, no new code (Line E + swift.jl glue script).
candidates.cost_estimate: month 1-2: 2-param (cD, cE) chiral-3NF emulator over n+d below breakup with realistic NN (extends swift.jl T3 to AV18 / chiral and reuses Line E infrastructure); month 3-6: full 5-param chiral-3NF posterior (cD, cE, c1, c3, c4); month 7-9: extend above breakup using complex scaling (swift.jl already has this); month 10-12: experimental-data confrontation + writing.

info_gain.if_succeeds: PRL that the entire FRIB-TA + Bochum + Pisa community has been actively trying to write since 2020. Punchline matters to chiral-EFT, ab initio, and the entire LRP §4.6 emulator-Bayesian agenda. Lei's Line E + swift.jl pair is uniquely positioned because nobody else has both a realistic-NN R-space Faddeev code AND a mature DBMM-emulator stack.
info_gain.if_fails: If the 5-param posterior comes out broad / uninformative, that's a separate PRC on 'why scattering observables don't constrain chiral 3NF well' — also high-value. Method paper on emulator-on-Faddeev is in-the-bag as PRC regardless.

candidates.risk: **Highest scoop risk in this set.** FRIB-TA emulator group (Furnstahl-Phillips-Drischler), Bochum (Witala-Skibinski), Pisa (Marcucci-Viviani-Girlanda) are all actively working on this. König 2020 was bound state; the scattering jump is the obvious next move. Lei's edge is owning BOTH the emulator and the Faddeev code as single-PI; competitors have to assemble teams. Estimated head-start: 6-12 months but shrinking. Lei needs to move FAST.
candidates.orthogonality_to_tried: Not T2 / T3 (extends them, but PRL handle is the posterior, not the Faddeev). Not T7 (T7 is DBMM-emulator-for-CDCC; this is DBMM-emulator-for-Faddeev which is a separate paper). Not T11 (orthogonal — no 4N).
candidates.orthogonality_to_already_read: König 2020 = bound state only; this is scattering. Zhang-Furnstahl 2022 = 1-parameter demonstration with toy NN; this is 5-parameter chiral with realistic NN. Carbonell 2013 §3 explicitly flags this gap.
candidates.dependencies: SLAM.jl Line E emulator (Lei owns), chiral-3NF Fortran kernel (Bochum / Idaho / LENPIC distributions exist), swift.jl chiral-NN integration (1-month engineering).
candidates.competitor_scoop_window: FRIB-TA Bayesian / emulator group ~6-12 month head-start risk; mitigated by Lei's unique code stack. Bochum 6-12 months. Pisa 6-12 months. Recommend: announce intent at a 2026 fall workshop to mark territory.

candidates.one_line: Compute and predict a previously-uncharacterized 3-body virtual state or sub-threshold resonance in a specific halo / mirror system using swift.jl + complex scaling, with an experimental signature accessible at FRIB/RIBLL within 2-3 years.
candidates.silent_assumption_violated: Assumes the PRL handle is a methodological achievement. Diagonal move: the handle is a PHYSICS PREDICTION — a new pole in a measurable channel — and swift.jl is the unique tool that can find it because Lazauskas-Carbonell complex-scaling Faddeev sees virtual states / sub-threshold resonances that P-space AGS struggles with.
candidates.prl_handle: I predict an unobserved low-lying near-threshold virtual state / sub-threshold resonance in the [n-n-α / n-³H / p-p-He / specific Borromean / mirror] 3-body continuum, with quantum numbers J^π and pole position computed from configuration-space Faddeev with realistic NN + chiral 3NF, and identify the kinematic signature that distinguishes it from background in low-energy nucleon-deuteron / α-particle invariant-mass spectra.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Today: scan swift.jl bound-state plus complex-scaling continuum for low-lying poles in candidate 3-body systems Lei can already compute (n-n-α via 4-body reduction, p-p-α, p-p-³He as A=5 mirror to t-t-³H, or the d-α / n-d virtual-state revisit). Pick the most experimentally-relevant candidate where Lei has a unique computational handle. One-day scan with existing swift.jl + complex-scaling infrastructure.
candidates.cost_estimate: month 1-2: candidate-pole scan across feasible 3-body targets, narrow to one system with experimental hook; month 3-5: realistic-NN + 3NF convergence of the pole; month 6-8: extract the experimental signature (cross-section shape, angular distribution, polarization observable) the experimentalists need to look for; month 9-11: contact FRIB / RIBLL / RIKEN experimentalists; month 12: write.

info_gain.if_succeeds: PRL with a concrete prediction — the gold standard for theory PRLs. Audience: nuclear-structure + reaction + RIB experimental communities all simultaneously. Sets up a multi-year experimental program. Cites in NSAC 2023 LRP / NuPECC 2024 LRP narratives.
info_gain.if_fails: If no new pole is found in any candidate system, the negative result (precision exclusion of low-lying virtual states in halo continuum) is a high-quality PRC. Halo-EFT community still wants this map.

candidates.risk: Predictive PRLs are high-variance: must beat the literature on the same system, AND survive experimental confirmation timing (could be scooped by a fast experiment). Mitigated by choosing a system where existing theoretical predictions are inconsistent (a 'theory dispute' angle, like the n-n-α virtual state history). Risk: Kievsky-Viviani or Lazauskas himself may have a draft.
candidates.orthogonality_to_tried: Not T1 / T5 (T1, T5 are 6Li-as-d+α universality at the bound state pole; this is the continuum pole). Not T2 / T3 (extends them but the PRL handle is the new prediction, not the Faddeev result). Not T11 (no 4N required for most candidate systems).
candidates.orthogonality_to_already_read: Kato CSGF only did ⁶He / ¹¹Li E1 strength with simple potentials, not realistic NN + 3NF pole searches in adjacent systems. Lazauskas-Carbonell 2012 did n-³H above 4N breakup with MT, not pole search. Halo-EFT literature has bounds but not realistic-NN poles.
candidates.dependencies: swift.jl complex-scaling infrastructure (have), realistic-NN engineering (T3 upgrade), 3-body channel-coupling for the chosen system, possibly Phillips / Hammer halo-EFT cross-check.
candidates.competitor_scoop_window: Lazauskas himself is the highest scoop risk because he has the same code line in production for 20 years. Hammer / Phillips / Ji halo-EFT line may have semi-quantitative predictions but not realistic-NN. Recommend: pick a candidate system where Lazauskas has NOT published in the last 5 years — that's the safe lane.

candidates.one_line: Cross-line PRL using swift.jl + Line F absorption-mechanism framework: an exact 3-body Faddeev decomposition of σ_abs into σ_fusion vs σ_W components for a benchmark deuteron-induced reaction, exposing the ~5-57% bias the off-diagonal-W simplification introduces and resolving a 50-year empirical-optical-potential ambiguity.
candidates.silent_assumption_violated: Assumes Line F is Liu Hao's PRC / PLB territory and swift.jl is unrelated. Diagonal move: use swift.jl to provide the exact Faddeev ground truth that Liu Hao + Lei + Ren's CDCC-level optical-theorem decomposition (PRC 113 054601 + PLB 877) has been asking for. The 5-57% bias becomes a PRL-tier finding when it is shown to be UNAVOIDABLE in standard reaction analyses.
candidates.prl_handle: I show via exact 3-body Faddeev calculations that the standard reaction-theory decomposition of the absorption cross section into fusion and breakup components is systematically biased by 20-50% across deuteron-induced reactions on light targets, with consequences for the entire literature on deuteron-induced surrogate reactions, (d,p) transfer cross sections, and fusion-evaporation modeling.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Today: pick the simplest deuteron-induced reaction Lei's swift.jl can solve exactly with charged channels (d+α elastic + breakup), compute σ_abs from swift.jl and compare to SMOOTHIE / Line F CDCC decomposition at the same energy. One day on existing infrastructure (deuteron is already swift.jl's natural object, see T3).
candidates.cost_estimate: month 1-2: d+α benchmark (swift.jl Faddeev vs SMOOTHIE CDCC); month 3-5: d+light-target systematic (12C, 16O); month 6-8: identify the kinematic regime where the bias is irreducible; month 9-10: surrogate / (d,p) implication mapping; month 11-12: write.

info_gain.if_succeeds: PRL that hits BOTH nuclear-reaction-theory and nuclear-astrophysics-surrogate communities. Audience is much broader than Faddeev specialists. Bridges Line F and the swift.jl branch in a way that closes Liu Hao's thesis.
info_gain.if_fails: If the bias is smaller than Liu Hao + Lei predicted, that's a PRC validation of the Line F approximations and a useful corrective. The d+light-target Faddeev benchmark itself is a good PRC.

candidates.risk: d+α is 3-body (d + α with α as inert core); fully realistic only if α excitation is negligible — for d+12C / d+16O the inert-core approximation may break. Could end up needing 4-body Faddeev-Yakubovsky, which violates the no-new-infrastructure rule. Mitigated by sticking to d+α with the inert-α validated cleanly.
candidates.orthogonality_to_tried: Not T8 (T8 is CDCC-level, this is Faddeev-level ground truth FOR T8). Not T4 (T4 is IAV-DWBA, distinct framework). Not T2 / T3 (extends them with the Line F overlay).
candidates.orthogonality_to_already_read: Liu Hao + Lei + Ren PRC 113 054601 and PLB 877 explicitly use CDCC, not Faddeev. The exact-Faddeev validation has never been done — Carbonell 2013 review does not cite Line F because Line F did not exist. Distinct from Deltuva-Fonseca because Lisbon does AGS in P-space, not the CDCC-vs-Faddeev bridge.
candidates.dependencies: Liu Hao + Ren collaboration extension (in-house, low friction). swift.jl realistic-NN deuteron-induced 3-body Faddeev with charged channels (T3 upgrade, p+d already in code).
candidates.competitor_scoop_window: Deltuva-Fonseca could do this in P-space AGS but has not published the Line-F-style decomposition in their 2024-2026 output (literature-search confirmation needed). Lazauskas could but has not focused on deuteron-induced reactions. Liu Hao's PRC 054601 + PLB 877 establish Lei's group's narrative ownership of the optical-theorem decomposition; this protects the lane. 6+ month safety window.
